{"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"cooking_tip","confidence":0.95,"matched_text":"1. **Low and Slow**: Cook your ribs low and slow (225-250°F) for 4-6 hours to allow the smoke to penetrate the meat.","distilled_fact":"Cooking ribs low and slow at 225-250°F for 4-6 hours allows smoke to penetrate the meat."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"cooking_tip","confidence":0.94,"matched_text":"2. **Wood Chip Selection**: Choose the right wood chips for the type of meat you're cooking. For example, hickory is great for pork ribs.","distilled_fact":"Hickory is great for pork ribs."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"cooking_tip","confidence":0.93,"matched_text":"3.
